commit.message:
feat: make DSC plugins optional with command line switches for execution (#11)

* feat: make DSC plugins optional with command line switches for execution

* Apply suggestions from code review

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: update DSC CLI options to make restoring and repackaging modules optional

* feat: add null check for CLI options and log warning for skipped plugin registration

* feat: enhance RestoreRequiredModulesPlugin with process management and timeout handling

* Improve PowerShell script execution cross-platform support

Previously, the plugin always used "powershell.exe" for script execution, limiting compatibility to Windows. Now, it dynamically selects the PowerShell executable based on the OS: "powershell.exe" for Windows and "pwsh" for other platforms. This change uses RuntimeInformation and a new PowerShellExecutable property to ensure scripts run correctly on all supported systems.

* Improve logging for module restore errors and timeouts

Enhanced error messages for required module restoration:
- Added distinct log messages for timeouts and unhandled exceptions.
- Updated resource strings and accessors in LogMessages.
- Improved error context in RestoreRequiredModulesPlugin for easier troubleshooting.

* Improve CLI argument parsing error handling

Updated argument parsing to use MapResult instead of directly accessing .Value, preventing exceptions on parse failure and improving robustness.

* Add Windows OS check and improve PSModulePath handling

RestoreRequiredModulesPlugin now verifies it is running on Windows before proceeding, logging an error and aborting on other platforms. Also, PSModulePath is now constructed using Path.PathSeparator for better platform compatibility, though the plugin is now Windows-only.

* Update script path construction for reliability

Switched from using Assembly.GetExecutingAssembly().Location and Path.Join to AppContext.BaseDirectory and Path.Combine when building the path to the "RestoreRequiredModules" PowerShell script. This change improves the reliability and correctness of script location across different deployment scenarios.

* Refactor plugin for testability; mock process in tests

Refactored RestoreRequiredModulesPlugin to introduce virtual methods for platform and process checks, enabling easier mocking in tests. Updated tests to override these methods, removing reliance on actual script files and process execution. Eliminated test helpers for script file management, resulting in more isolated and side-effect-free unit tests.

* Fix spacing in RepackageRequiredModules property declaration

Removed extra space before opening brace in the RepackageRequiredModules property of DscCliOptions for consistent formatting.

commit.message:
feat(start): HITL gate after gate1 green to choose next action (#49)

* feat: start: HITL gate after gate1 green to choose next action (#48)

Add step 18 to commands/start.md so the green-handoff moment is no
longer a silent return-to-prompt. After the recap and implementation
guidance, /start now prints a Considerations block (gate1 verdict,
top suggestions, scope, available skills, memory signal, caveats) and
fires an AskUserQuestion with three options: Stop here / Continue with
the suggested action (auto-launch /feature-dev when detected, draft a
plan otherwise) / Free-form feedback. red still aborts at step 12;
yellow keeps its existing confirm; DRY_RUN skips step 18 entirely.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Update commands/start.md

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: start: reframe step 17 as data-prep layer for step 18 HITL gate (#48)

Step 17's intro at commands/start.md:674 used to claim "this step closes
the perceived workflow gap" — accurate when step 17 was the terminal step
of /start, but misleading after PR #49 added step 18, which actually owns
the green-handoff moment now. Reword to credit step 18 explicitly and
name the substeps (17a key suggestions, 17b skill detection, 17c
suggested workflow) whose outputs step 18 reuses in its Considerations
block. DX-Lead-approved one-line replacement, no behavior change.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: start: address Copilot review round 2 — 18c rules + 18d/e flow (#48)

Three substantive fixes from Copilot's review of #9670b69:

1. 18c Caveats bullet: tighten the rule. Caveats must be EXPLICITLY
   recorded upstream as flags or variables (e.g. yellow carry-forward
   from step 12, force flag from arg parsing). Inference from derived
   values is forbidden — in particular, the previous example "branch
   was auto-suffixed because the original name collided" is removed
   because no earlier step records a BRANCH_COLLISION flag, so 18c
   would have had to infer it from BRANCH alone, violating the
   "do not invent new analysis here" rule at 18c's intro.

2. 18c Skills bullet: align with step 17b's "omit silently — do not
   mention unavailable skills" rule (line 698). Previously 18c said to
   "name what option 2 will fall back to (Draft an implementation plan
   now)" when /feature-dev was missing, which indirectly mentions the
   missing skill. The continue target's label (set in 18b) already
   conveys what option 2 will do, so operators do not need to be told
   what is missing. Repo convention preserved.

3. 18d/18e: drop the dependency on AskUserQuestion's "Other" / inline
   free-form mode. No other section of this repo uses such a mode
   (step 12 yellow-confirm and commands/doctor.md:140-146 Copilot setup
   both use fixed options only) and its portability across Claude Code
   environments is not documented. Restructured option 3 as a fixed
   selection that switches into a follow-up conversational turn: select
   option 3 → 18e prints an invitation acknowledgement → /start stops
   and waits → operator types feedback as their next message → Claude
   responds conversationally to that next message. Same operator
   experience, no new tool-capability assumption.

All three were caught by Copilot reviewer (PR #49 review round 2 inline
comments at line 749, 751, 782). Independently confirmed valid before
applying. CTO self-review missed all three; lessons-learned noted in
the PR description.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: readline の二重エコーバグを修正（terminal: false を設定）

readline.createInterface に terminal: false を追加し、readline 自身の
エコー処理を無効化する。

## 原因
readUserInput が setRawMode(true) + 手動エコーで文字を stdout に書き込む一方、
readline.Interface も terminal: true の場合に同じ文字をエコーするため、
/clear のような入力が //cclleeaarr と二重表示されていた。

- src/index.ts: readline.createInterface に terminal: false を指定
- rl.question() 呼び出し時点では raw モードが解除されており OS 側がエコーを

Chunk 8: Add forgot/reset password flow

- Add update_password() to DatabaseManager (hashes and saves new password)
- Add get_reset_token() / verify_reset_token() using itsdangerous
  URLSafeTimedSerializer with a 1-hour expiry
- Add /auth/forgot-password route: sends reset link via email; always
  shows a generic message to avoid leaking whether the email exists
- Add /auth/reset-password/<token> route: validates token, enforces
  8-char minimum, checks confirmation match, updates password
- Add forgot_password.html and reset_password.html templates
  (reset form has live JS password-match validation)
- Add 'Forgot password?' link below the password field on login.html

fix(nginx/varnish): eliminate intermittent "server error on every other page request" (#9)

* fix(nginx/varnish): eliminate intermittent server error on every other page request

Three cooperating bugs caused the consistent "every other request fails /
refresh works" pattern with no error log entries:

1. nginx proxy_next_upstream only covered TCP error/timeout — Varnish
   synthetic 503s and WordPress 500s were forwarded to the browser without
   a retry. Add http_500/502/503/504 so nginx transparently retries.
   POST requests are safe: nginx never retries if the body was already sent.

2. Varnish briefly cached HTML error pages (beresp.ttl=5s). WordPress's
   fatal-error handler sends Cache-Control: no-cache only for logged-in
   users; anonymous 5xx pages had no Cache-Control header and were stored,
   causing every request within that 5-second window to receive the cached
   error. Change to beresp.uncacheable=true so each request gets a fresh
   backend attempt.

3. No vcl_backend_error handler. During stale-while-revalidate background
   fetches a transient connection failure replaced the good cached object
   with an error. Add vcl_backend_error: return(abandon) for bgfetch
   preserves the cached object; return(retry) for foreground misses
   attempts one reconnect before synthesising an error.

Signed-off-by: Max Barrett <34328348+MaximillianGroup@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(varnish): scope hit-for-miss to 5xx only; keep short TTL for 3xx/4xx

The previous fix incorrectly applied beresp.uncacheable = true to all
non-2xx HTML responses (including 3xx redirects and 4xx errors), removing
even brief caching for stable redirects and common 404 pages and
unnecessarily increasing origin load during crawls.

Split the non-2xx branch into three cases:
- 2xx: 10m TTL + 60s stale-while-revalidate grace (unchanged)
- 5xx (>= 500 && < 600): beresp.uncacheable = true, ttl=5s — hit-for-miss
  so every request gets a fresh backend attempt. Prevents anonymous
  WordPress fatal-error pages (which carry no Cache-Control header) from
  being briefly cached and served to subsequent requests within the window.
- 3xx/4xx: 5s TTL, no grace — brief caching absorbs redirect/404 traffic
  spikes without hammering origin, and expires quickly when content changes.

Bug 1 (P0): Plan generation fails silently on first install because
exercise seed data loads asynchronously and may not be ready when
completeOnboarding() runs. Added retry loop (5 attempts, 500ms delay)
in OnboardingViewModel and a require() guard in WorkoutPlanGenerator
that throws when exercises are empty.

confirmation dialog. Added BackHandler composable to intercept system
back and show the existing discard dialog instead of navigating away.

* fix: add kotlin-compose plugin to core module — fixes localizedName() runtime crash

Without the Compose compiler plugin, @Composable extension functions in
core module compiled but generated incorrect bytecode. This caused
NoSuchMethodError at runtime when feature module tried to invoke them.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: onboarding plan generation + back button discard confirmation

Bug 1 (P0): Plan generation fails silently on first install because
exercise seed data loads asynchronously and may not be ready when
completeOnboarding() runs. Added retry loop (5 attempts, 500ms delay)
in OnboardingViewModel and a require() guard in WorkoutPlanGenerator
that throws when exercises are empty.

Bug 2 (P1): Hardware back button on Active Workout bypassed the discard
confirmation dialog. Added BackHandler composable to intercept system
back and show the existing discard dialog instead of navigating away.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: final polish — plan confirmation, hardcoded string sweep, a11y

- Post-onboarding: show snackbar 'Your N-day plan is ready! 🎉' when plan
  generates successfully after onboarding (bilingual EN/ES)
- Hardcoded string sweep: replaced all remaining user-facing literal strings
  in OnboardingViewModel, SettingsViewModel, and ProfileViewModel with
  context.getString(R.string.xxx) backed by bilingual string resources
- A11y: added contentDescription to 'View all programs' clickable text and
  mergeDescendants semantics to RecentWorkoutCard for screen readers
- Verified Redo Setup in Settings correctly clears onboarding and navigates
- Updated OnboardingViewModelTest for new constructor and effect signatures

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: ensure seed data available before plan generation in onboarding

Replace the fragile .first() + require() pattern in WorkoutPlanGenerator
with .first { isNotEmpty() } wrapped in withTimeout(30s). Room's reactive
Flow re-emits when the async seed INSERT commits, so the generator now
reliably suspends until exercises are available instead of failing
immediately on cold first-install.

Closes #464

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: use INSERT OR IGNORE for exercise seed to prevent constraint crash

commit.message:
Add Converse API support for non-Anthropic models (#13)

Route non-Anthropic Bedrock models (Amazon Nova, Meta Llama, Mistral,
Cohere, DeepSeek, AI21 Jamba, etc.) through the Bedrock Converse API
while keeping Anthropic Claude on the native Messages API.

New traits:
- DetectsModelApi: routes by model family (anthropic.* vs others)
- BuildsConverseRequests: Converse request format (inferenceConfig,
  toolConfig with toolSpec, system prompts)
- ParsesConverseResponses: Converse response parsing (output.message,
  camelCase usage, tool call execution loop)
- HandlesConverseStreaming: Converse stream events (messageStart,
  contentBlockDelta, etc.) with tool call support

Features supported via Converse API:
- Text generation and streaming
- Tool use / function calling
- Structured output (synthetic tool approach)
- System prompts
- Provider-specific options (additionalModelRequestFields)

commit.message:
robustness: harden controller, fix DST, delete dead code

Sleep Controller v4:
- Room temp plausibility guard (reject values outside 40-100°F range)
- Override floor clamped to MAX_SETTING=0 (user can't set heating floor)
- DST-safe elapsed time: store sleep_start_epoch, use for cycle math
- Merge override_freeze_until into override_floor_ts (simpler state)
- Delete _ensure_schema() migration (run one-time scripts instead)
- Fix Postgres controller_version column (was defaulting to 'v3')
- Remove dead fallback sensor reads in _log_to_postgres
- Proper Postgres connection cleanup (close before clearing)

Config:

commit.message:
Fix scan failures: use -OutputFile to avoid PowerShell stream leaking to stdout (#69)

* Initial plan

* Fix scan failures: Write-Host leaks to stdout in non-interactive subprocess mode

Root cause: Write-Host in PowerShell uses the Information stream (stream 6),
which flows to the process's stdout when pwsh runs as a non-interactive
subprocess. Since bash redirects stdout to scan.json.tmp, any Write-Host
output prepends "WARNING:" text to the JSON, causing parse failures
('Unexpected character at position 0: W').

Fix: Replace all Write-Host diagnostic calls in Get-PrTriageData.ps1 with
[Console]::Error.WriteLine(), which writes directly to fd 2 (stderr).
Update ScriptHygiene.Tests.ps1 to ban Write-Host in Get-PrTriageData.ps1
and recommend [Console]::Error.WriteLine() instead.

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/danmoseley/pr-dashboard/sessions/83d68ddc-2d80-43f6-b6a9-40c0ac151fb1

Co-authored-by: danmoseley <6385855+danmoseley@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update Write-Output test message to recommend [Console]::Error.WriteLine()

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Use -OutputFile param instead of stdout redirection to fix stream leaking

PowerShell's Write-Warning (stream 3) and Write-Host (stream 6) leak to
stdout when pwsh runs as a subprocess from bash. Instead of working around
this with [Console]::Error.WriteLine(), the script now writes its output
file directly via -OutputFile, so stdout is never redirected and standard
PowerShell cmdlets work naturally.

- Add -OutputFile parameter; Emit-Output helper writes to file or stdout
- Revert [Console]::Error.WriteLine() back to idiomatic Write-Warning
- Use Write-Host for GH Actions ::warning:: annotations (needs exact prefix)
- Guard catch block against double-failure if file write fails
- Use UTF-8 without BOM for JSON output
- Update workflow to use -OutputFile instead of > redirect
- Replace Write-Warning/Write-Host bans with stdout-redirect ban in tests
- Update copilot-instructions.md local testing example

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix test -Because message to reflect -OutputFile approach

Enhanced CI/CD: 3 failure scenarios + build-test PR pipeline

Deploy pipeline (PowerGrid-Deploy):
- failure_scenario parameter: none, crash, perf, config, all
- crash: FORCE_ERROR=true on outage-api → 503 errors
- perf: SIMULATE_DELAY_MS=5000 on grid-status-api → 5s latency
- config: removes REQUIRED_CONFIG from notification-svc → crash loop
- all: injects all three simultaneously

CI pipeline (PowerGrid-BuildTest):
- Triggers on PRs to main
- Builds all services, runs inline tests
- Validates no failure env vars are set in code
- SRE Agent creates fix PR → CI runs → green → merge → clean deploy

End-to-end SRE Agent flow:
1. Run deploy with failure_scenario=crash
2. Alert fires → SRE Agent investigates
3. SRE Agent finds root cause via ADO pipeline run correlation
4. SRE Agent creates fix PR (CreateFixPullRequest tool)
5. CI pipeline validates the fix
6. Merge → deploy pipeline runs clean → services healthy
